purpose: Pin the contract for the profile-gated RCU/BRAVO-class read-mostly fast-path prototype produced by `frankenlibc_harness::read_mostly_fast_path_prototype`. The optimization changes EXACTLY ONE LEVER — the lane the reader takes (`Conservative` mutex vs. `Seqlock`). Both lanes share the same writer protocol and write to the same backing store, so the optimization can be reverted by flipping the profile flag back to `Conservative` without invalidating any unrelated evidence artifact. This manifest gates (1) the isomorphism proof note any future live experiment must respect, (2) the deterministic-fallback contract, and (3) the schema of the live before/after measurement run.

selected_hotspot.rationale: The bd-juvqm.2 attribution gate's `tls_cache` stage is read-mostly by construction: writers are rare (cache invalidations) and readers are every membrane validation. Reducing the read latency at this stage compounds across every API call. No new synchronization abstraction is invented — the prototype uses the seqlock primitive whose bounded-soundness floor was proved in bd-juvqm.8.

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Both lanes observe writes in program order: the conservative lane via mutex acquire/release; the seqlock lane via Acquire-loaded version sandwiched around an Acquire-loaded value. Every successful seqlock read corresponds to the same instant the mutex would have observed.
isomorphism_proof.visibility: Writers update the seqlock value (with version-bump bracketing) BEFORE releasing the mutex. A reader on either lane therefore observes either the pre-write or the post-write value — never a torn intermediate.
isomorphism_proof.safety_state_monotonicity: The membrane SafetyState is not modified by either reader; only the value field is. Therefore neither lane changes the safety-state lattice.
isomorphism_proof.errno_abi_behavior: Neither lane returns errno or modifies the libc errno location. Reader signature is `fn read(&self) -> u32`; behavior is identical across lanes.
isomorphism_proof.strict_hardened_divergence: The strict/hardened mode distinction does NOT affect this reader — the value field is the same in both modes. The bd-juvqm.12 timing side-channel budget gate covers any p99 delta arising from lane choice; this prototype must keep that delta within the per-path cap (200ns for pointer.validate_region, the surface that consumes tls_cache reads).

deterministic_fallback_contract.rationale: The Conservative mutex lane is always available, always returns the most recently published value, and never retries. Switching `profile_lane` from Seqlock back to Conservative at any time produces an outcome sequence identical to a run that started in Conservative.
deterministic_fallback_contract.single_lever_invariant: The ONLY observable change between profiles is read latency. Published values, write protocol, and diagnostic counters are identical.

live_measurement_schema.rationale: When a future live experiment produces before/after p99/p999/throughput measurements, every row MUST satisfy this schema. The validator at `frankenlibc_harness::read_mostly_fast_path_prototype::validate_live_measurement` enforces it.

live_measurement_runner.rationale: Resolves the gap left in the original bd-juvqm.3 contract: the schema was pinned but no runner produced rows. `frankenlibc_harness::read_mostly_fast_path_prototype::run_live_measurement_pair` drives both lanes under identical anchoring inputs and emits a `LiveMeasurementPair`.

deterministic_write_pattern.rationale: Writers are rare relative to readers — read-mostly hotspot characterization. Each `write_period`-th read triggers a write of `seed^i`.

downstream_consumers.consumes: Strict/hardened timing side-channel budget gate — lane choice MUST keep the per-path p99 delta within the 200ns cap on pointer.validate_region (the surface that consumes tls_cache reads).
Reuses the bounded model-check soundness floor for the seqlock primitive.

summary.next_safe_action: Run the live before/after experiment under bd-juvqm.1's high-core tail baseline harness; emit a LiveMeasurementRow for each (lane, profile) pair; the gate will validate fields and refuse to promote unless the lanes agree on the isomorphism witness AND the strict/hardened timing side-channel budget (bd-juvqm.12) holds.
